def: s.f. = ṭikānī, q.v. s.f. A prop for the support of the shafts or pole of a carriage (cf. tek).
n. f. A prop for the support of the shafts or pole of a carriage.
s.m. A system of ćaukī-dārī prevailing in certain parts of India (as in Rohtak Borī), the duty of keeping the village watch (taken by the villagers in turn; = thīkar).
n. The system of chaukī-dārī prevailing in Rohtak Berī.
s.m. The duty of keeping the village watch (taken in turn by the villagers).

s.m. = ṭhīkrā, q.v. s.m. The duty of keeping the village watch (taken in turn by the villagers).

def: n. m. H. ṭukṛā a piece. 1. Potsherd; shard; a broken piece of any earthen-ware. 1. Hāth meṅ ṭhīkrā hogā, aur bhīk māṅgtā phiregā . A curse. A potsherd in your hand, at every door a beggar stand! ☞ Gālib, the witty poet of Delhi, relates that one day, as his servant was murmuring over the chilam which he filled with live coals from the ṭhīkrā , (fire place, also potsherd) for his master's hubble-bubble, he enquired what he had been saying to the ṭhīkrā potsherd. The servant replied that he had been saying 'how he had had no pay for eight months!' And what did the potsherd say?' He said 'Dont mind, I am with you' (' Ghabrā nahīṅ, maiṅ tere sāth hūṅ .') 2. Hāth meṅ ṭhīkrā, aur ṭhikre meṅ sattar chhed! Wom! May you beg from door to door, with a broken earthen pot full of holes (through which the alms you get may fall out)! 2. A broken vessel or ornament; a dilapidated house. 3. ( Contemp .) A vessel; a house. 4. Houses and lands. 5. An earthen pot for holding fire, filth, etc. ṭhīkrā phoṛnā , v. a. To slander one. buzurgoṅ kā ṭhīkrā , n. m. Houses or lands descended from one's ancestors by hereditary right. khāne, kamāne, yā rozī kā ṭhīkrā , n. m. 1. The tools or instruments by which one lives. 2. A girl brought up by the mother bawd.
s.m. The duty of keeping the village watch (taken in turn by the villagers).

s.m. A broken piece of earthenware, a large potsherd, a shard; a vessel for holding fire (such as faqīrs use, — usually the lower half of an earthen pitcher); — an earthen vessel for holding filth, &c.; a broken vessel or ornament; a dilapidated house; houses and lands
more: s. m. A broken piece of earthen ware.
subs: (-kā), (fig.) To slander, to defame; — rozī-kā ṭhīkrā, kamāne-kā ṭhīkrā, s.m. The tools, instruments, or means by which one lives.

def: s.f. = ṭhīkrī, q.v.; — also = ṭikrī, q.v.
more: s. f. Potsherd, shard: see ٿهيکري ṭhīkrī. ٿهکري مار ṭhikrī-mār (dakh.) A game amongst children, throwing a fragment of an earthen vessel so as to glide along the surface of the water ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: n. f. 1. Dim. of ṭhīkrā , q. v. 2. The upper or moveable jaw of the hammer of a musket. 3. Mons veneris .
s. (dakh.) Potsherd: see ٿهيکري ṭhīkrī.
s.f. A broken piece of earthen ware, potsherd; an earthen pan (for baking bread); the upper or movable jaw of the cock of a musket; mons veneris
more: s. f. 1. A broken piece of earthen ware, a potsherd. 2. Mons veneris. ٿهيکري چنا ṭhīkrī chunnā, To be mad.

s. f. (dakh.) Two bits of stick or fragments of earthenware used as castanets: or, two pieces of wood, one held between the fore and middle finger, the other between the middle and ring finger of the left hand; while, with the thumb and middle finger of the right hand, the ends of the sticks on the outside of the hand are made to strike against each other, producing the same sound as castanets ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s. m. Exactitude.
s. f. (dakh.) Purse, bag: see تهيلي thailī.
s.f. An earthen waterpot or pitcher
subs: s.m. lit. 'Filling a pitcher'; the ceremony of offering a libation of water to the manes of the deceased.

def: n. f. H. thal place. An earthen pitcher. Tāo ke din bā, ek ṭhilyā pānī meṅ kā hokhī? Bhoj. The day is warm, one pitcher of water is not enough. ṭhilyā bharnā , Hin. n. m. lit . To fill a pitcher with water. The rite of pouring a libation of water to the manes of the dead.
s.f. Sob; whimper, fretful cry (of a child).
v.n. To sob; to cry, whimper, fret (as a child); to whine, complain, murmur.
more: v. n. To sob. ¶ ṭhanaknā, v. n. 1. To throb, to shoot (as, the pain of headach). 2. To jingle, tinkle, clink.

adj. (f. -ī), Of small stature, short, dwarfish, diminutive (syn. nāṭā).

def: adj. Of small stature; middle sized.
s.f. Lowness of stature, dwarfishness, &c.
s.f. A vessel for holding or carrying fire (such as faqīrs use, — usually the lower half of an earthen pitcher, syn. ṭhikrā); a brasier, chafing-dish.
more: s. f. A kind of lamp.

def: adj. Fixed, firm, stable, permanent, durable; settled; tranquil, calm, smooth, even
more: adj. S. स्थिर See اتَل (1—4, 6, 7.) Sadā na joban thir rahe, sadā na sāvan hoë, Sadā na phūle ketkī, sadā na bar meṅ joë . Dohā. Not ever will your beauty last, not ever August rains will fall! Not always blooms the Kelkī flower, nor mistress loved your arms enthrall!
subs: v.n. To continue fixed or firm; to be durable, to last, &c.

def: adj. = thir, q.v. (vulg. or poetical). adj. Fixed, firm, stable, permanent, durable; settled; tranquil, calm, smooth, even
subs: v.n. To continue fixed or firm; to be durable, to last, &c.

def: s.f. Extreme cold; frost (cf. ṭhār; ṭhiṭhar).
adj. Difficult, hard, untoward, trying
subs: s.m. A hard or trying time.

def: v.n. To be set, be settled, be well postured (in dancing, &c.); to move or dance in a stately manner, or with expressive action and gesture; to strut, stalk; to mince, dance.
more: v. n. To mince; dance; shake; flourish; quiver; dance with expressive action and gesture. Wom. Zanān mantriyoṅ kī tarah thiraktā phirtā hai .
v. n. To set oneʼs self off well in dancing, to dance with expressive action and gesture.
v. n. (dakh) To hover (as a bird: Harris ).

def: v.n. = thiraknā, q.v. v.n. To be set, be settled, be well postured (in dancing, &c.); to move or dance in a stately manner, or with expressive action and gesture; to strut, stalk; to mince, dance.

more: (caus. of ٿهرنا) v. a. To cause to congeal. ¶ ṭharānā (caus. of ٿهرنا) v. a. To fix, settle; see ٿهـﮩرانا.

def: s.f. Firmness, stability, motionlessness, stillness, rest; steadfastness; settlement; calm, quiet, tranquillity, serenity.
more: n. f. 1. Rest; stillness. 2. Quiet; tranquility; calm; serenity.
s. f. Rest, settlement, tranquillity, steadfastness.

v. n. To be chilled; to freeze.
v.n. To freeze; to be chilled.

v. n. 1. To be numbed, benumbed, chilled; to shiver. 2. To be stopped (growth); nipped in the bud. 1. Jāṛā ke māre hāth, goṛ ṭhiṭhural jā le . Mag. 2. Jāṛe meṅ paude ṭhiṭar jāte haiṅ .
s.f. The state of standing still, stagnation; the state of stopping suddenly, or of standing amazed, &c.; — (conj. part. of next), adv. Reluctantly, hesitatingly, slowly ( = ṭhiṭhak-ke).
more: (from ٿهٿهکنا) s. f. The state of standing amazed. ٿهٿهک رهنا يا جانا ṭhiṭhak-rahnā or -jānā, v. n. To stop short.

def: adv. In a state of amazement, staggered; reluctantly, shrinkingly, hesitatingly, slowly.

(In comp). Number. Do ṭho . Two.

s.m. Multitude, mass, quantity; clump, heap; body, band, company, party, community; amount, total sum; ready money; share, portion, allotment, lot; holding, tenure, local division (of an estate); a subdivision in coparcenary (bhaʼiyā ćārī) estate; a spot where three or more boundary lines meet, junction of boundaries
more: n. m. S. स्तोम multitude. 1. A company; class; party; community; band ( giroh ). 2. A total amount; a sum. 3. An allotted portion; a share; lot; portion. 4. A tenure; holding; a local division of an estate. 5. A sub-division in a bhaīyā chārī or coparcenary estate. 6. A point where three or more boundary lines meet.
s. m. 1. Amount, heap, ready money. 2. Share, portion. تهوک دار thok-dār, s. m. A wholesale dealer.
subs: (-kā), To form into parties or companies, &c.; to form into lots; to fix the limits of estates (by a native survey)
s.f. Fixing the limits of estates (by a native survey, or preparatory to a regular scientific survey), laying down and marking off boundaries (of an estate, &c.)
s.m. The head of a company, or community; the holder of a thok; the holder of a principa share (in a village, who is responsible for the payment of the revenue); — a wholesale dealer; a copyholder.

def: s.m. (dialec.), Blow, stroke, &c. ( = ṭahokā; ṭhokar).
s.m. (dialec.), Blow, stroke, &c. ( = ṭahokā; ṭhokar).
s.m. The act of making firm, the act of driving in (a peg, &c.), or of beating, or striking.

ety: rt. of ṭhoknā+ar = Prk. अडी = S. र+ई
def: s.f. A cause of stumbling (as projecting stones, boulders, &c. on a road), obstacle, stumbling-block; tripping or striking the foot against anything; trip, stumble, false step; kick, thump, blow, stroke; shock; loss (in trade, &c.); beating time with the foot; a cow-catcher (syn. ćhāj)
more: s. f. 1. Tripping or striking the foot against any thing, a beat of the foot, a kick, a stumble. 2. A stumblingblock. ٿهوکر کهانا ṭhokar khānā, 1. To trip, to stumble. 2. To meet with a loss or misfortune. ٿهوکرلگنا ṭhokar lagnā, To strike against the foot (a stumblingblock or obstacle).
subs: v.t. To kick away, to spurn
v.n. To trip, stumble; to meet with a loss, to suffer misfortune
v.n. To trip, &c. ( = ṭhokar khānā); to be kicked and knocked about, to be ill-used (by, -kī)
(-kī), To come into collision (with), to knock (against, an obstacle), to trip, stumble
To kick (against, -par), knock or strike (against); to strike, kick, spurn (with, -ko).

def: n. f. 1. Tripping or striking the foot accidentally against anything; a false step; a trip; stumble. 1. Ṭhokar khāve, budh pāve . Prov. From knocks received without good sense is driven within. 2. Jo sir uṭhā-ke chalega, so ṭhokar khāëgā . Prov. Who walk with head uplifted high will trip and prostrate lie. 2. A kick. 3. Projecting stones, etc. on the road; a bad road. Ṭhokar hai! (Kahārs' idiom). 4. Shock; blow; loss. Sau rupaë kī ṭhokar lagī . 5. A cow-catcher ( chhāj ). 6. Beating time with the foot. Banā-ke saj dhaj, phirā-ke dāman, lagā-ke ṭhokar, hilā-ke bālā . Nazīr. Her person all adorned, her skirt coquettish jerked, a mincing gait assumed, her earrings frequent shook. 7. A pair of shoes (Gamblers' idiom). ṭhokar par mārnā , v. n. To kick away; to spurn. ṭhokar khānā , v. n. 1. To stumble. 2. To suffer; sustain a loss. Ādmī ṭhokar khā-kar saṅbhaltā hai . Prov. Stumble and get sense. ṭhokaren khānā , v. n. 1. ( ṭhokar lagnā ). To trip; stumble. Ṭhokar lagī pahāṛ kī, aur toṛeṅ ghar kī sil . Prov. He hurts his foot against the rocks, the grinding stone to pieces knocks. (He vents his anger on his wife). 2. To be kicked, or made a foot ball of. Bin maiyā kā bachchā ṭhokreṅ khātā phirtā hai . Prov. Saying. The motherless child gets knocks and blows where'er he goes. 3. To be sat upon; to have to bear any harshness or insult. Kyoṅ apne parāyoṅ kī ṭhokreṅ khāūṅ? Wom. ṭhokar mārnā , v. n. 1. To strike; knock on the head; kick. 2. To kick away; turn up one's nose at.
n. f. Laying down and marking off the boundaries; fixing the limits of estates by a native survey, or preparatory to a professional survey. thok bāṅdhnā , v. a. To form into parties.
n. m. 1. The holder of a thok . 2. The head of a company or society. 3. A wholesale dealer; a copyholder.

ety: ṭhok˚ = Prk. टुक्ख(इ) = S. त्वक्ष(ति), rt. त्वक्ष्
def: v.t. To make firm, to drive in (a stake, peg, &c.); to hammer, strike, beat, thump, knock; to pat, tap, fillip; to thrust with the finger; to play, perform on (the kettle-drum); to put in the stocks; to enter (a complaint against), file (a suit or action)
more: v. a. To beat, to strike, to drive (as, a stake).
subs: adv. After striking and sounding, after close examination or scrutiny; openly, publicly
(-meṅ), To put a spoke (in one's wheel)
(-kā), To make a lock secure, to lock or shut up.

def: v. a. 1. To drive in (a stake, etc). Kīl ṭhoknā . 2. To bang; thwack; lay on; whallop. 3. To punish; serve out; castigate ( pīṭnā 2, 3). Masjid meṅ vāëzoṅ ke taīṅ lag gaī hai baṛ, Zāhik ne ṭhokā Shai/?/kh ko pagṛī utār-kar . Saudā. In mosque the priest his voice still sounds his doctrines to propound, The turban of the priest knocked off, his head the wag did sound. 4. To enter a suit or complaint against. 5. To put in the stocks. 6. To pat on the back. Kamar ṭhoṅknā. ṭhok bajā-ke , adv. 1. After close examination. 2. Openly; publicly. ṭhok bajā-ke lenā, ṭhok bajā-ke dekhnā , v. n. To sound (coin, crockery, etc.). 1. Dekhī ṭhok bajā-ke, dunyā tālib zar kī . Prov. The world on money-getting keen, its soundness oft assayed has been. 2. Damṛī kī hāṅḍī bhī ṭhok bajā-ke lete haiṅ . Prov. A farthing's earthen pot you buy, its soundness oft you sound and try. baṅgā ṭhoknā , v. a. 1. To put a spoke in one's wheel. 2. ( Slang ). To harrass; annoy. tālā ṭhoknā , v. a. To lock, shut, or seal up. arzī ṭhoknā , v. n. To file a suit or action.

def: v.t. To heap, pile, collect; to cover, hide; to cap, mount.
more: v. a. S. स्तूप to heap up. 1. To pat; put on the pan. Āṭā milā to īṅdhan, chūlhā, tavā na-dārad, Roṭī pakāve kis par, ghar meṅ tavā na-dārad, Gar ṭhīkre pe thope to phir mazā na-dārad, Nau chhed, peṅdī gāyab, jis par galā na-dārad, Pānī kā garmiyoṅ meṅ jhajjar milā to aisā! On Poverty. Nazīr. If chance bring him some flour, nor fuel nor hearth he's got, Nor baking-pan—on broken shard 'twould n't taste a jot— And if in summer's heat he find a water pot, Be sure, it has nine holes, nor neck nor bottom aught, Such are the windfalls in the luckless poor man's lot! 2. To apply with the fingers or palm; to clap on; plaster. 3. To cover; hide.
v. a. 1. To support, to prop. 2. To plaster. 3. To pile, to heap.

adj. 1. Few; some; a little; small; scanty. 1. Rāṅḍ kā sāṅḍ, saudāgar kā ghoṛā, khāë bahut, chale thoṛā . Prov. A widow's son and a dealer's horse eat much, move slow. 2. Bahut kaṭ gaī, thoṛī rah gaī . Prov. Saying. The best part of my life is gone, but little now remains. 2. Insufficient; inadequate; low; below par; short; under; less than. Apne piyā par tan man vārūṅ, jo vārūṅ so thoṛā re . Wom. Ṭhumrī . For my beloved I'll give my body and soul, give all I can how small is still the whole? 3. Narrow; limited; confined; bounded. 4. Slight; feeble; slender; trifling. Yeh thoṛī bāt nahīṅ hai . This is no trifling matter. 5. Scarce; rare; uncommon. thoṛā bahut , adj. More or less; about; a little. thoṛā thoṛā , adv. See ahistah , 2. Thoṛā thoṛā kar-ke bahut ho jātā hai . Prov. A little and a little more makes up at last a great big store. (Every mickle makes a muckle). thoṛā thoṛā h . v. n. To feel oneself small; to be covered with shame. thoṛā sā, thoṛī sī , adj. A little; a very little. Fikr-i-maāsh, ishq-i-butāṅ, yād-i-raftagāṅ, Thoṛī sī umr meṅ koī kyā kyā kīyā kare . The care to live, the pangs of love, the grief for those who've gone, In what brief space what wondrous things by one man seen and done! thoṛā k v. a. To lessen; decrease; diminish; reduce; mitigate. thoṛe dinoṅ se , adv. For a short time; temporarily; of late; recently. thoṛā hī, thoṛaī, thoṛāī , intj. Not in the least; not a bit of it; not a jot; never. 1. Maiṅ vahāṅ thoṛe hī thā . Not a bit of me was there at all. 2. Yahāṅ merā nāl thoṛā hī gaṛā hai . Is my navel string the least bit buried here? (What is there to keep me here?). ☞ It is the practice to bury the navel string in the house. Hence an attachment to the house is believed to arise.

more: intj. An imitative word for the sound of hawking and spitting ( tuf ). thū thū k . v. n. 1. To spit continually. Mujhe thū thū karnā achchhā nahīṅ lagtā . 2. To spit on or at; scorn; disdain; despise. Ek laṛkā janam kā hīnā, jin dekhā, un thū thī kīnā . Riddle. Ans. Ugāldān . A spittoon. A child unborn, spat on with scorn. thū thū honā , v. n. 1. To be derided, scorned, mocked at. 2. To be disgraced, dishonored; to be come a byword. Sārī birādrī meṅ thū thū ho rahī hai. thū thū, thū thū thuddā . An expression used by boys at play when one wants to fall out. 'Hamārī thū thū hai.' 'Thū thū kare, murdā khāë .' 'I want to go out.' 'Whoe'er goes out on carrion feeds about.'

def: v.n. To spit (at, -meṅ or -par), to treat with utter contempt; to vilify, abuse
more: v. a. To spit. 1. Koī ā-kar thūktā bhī nahī . Wom. No one would even spit in your house. 2. Tum thūkte ho. Ham thūkte bhī nahīṅ . You spit at me. I would n't even do that much at you. 3. Apnā ghar hag bhar, parāyā ghar thūk kā bhī ḍar . Prov. Your own house you may foul, another's you may not spit in. 2. To spit at; show the greatest contempt for one. Thūkeṅge sab log, lugāī, dekh, tere is mchṅḍe meṅ; Yeh augan tere wahīṅ chhipeṅge kisī gāoṅ aur goṅḍe meṅ . Play. Chaubolā. 3. To call names; talk about one; abuse. Muhalle ke log kyā thūkeṅge . Wom. khūn thūknā , v. n. To spit blood. thukkā fazīhatī ; Wom. tukkā fazītī . 1. Disgrace, abuse. 2. Quarrel; contention; strife.

or thūk ćāṭnā, v.n. To swallow one's own words, to retract or recall a promise, to break one's word
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀣𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀇 (thukkaï), from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀣𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀢𑀺 (*thukkati). Cognate with Gujarati થૂંકવું (thū̃kvũ), Marathi थुंकणे (thuṅkṇe), Sindhi ٿُڪَڻُ (thukaṇu) / थुकणु (thukaṇu), Punjabi ਥੁੱਕਣਾ (thukkaṇā), Nepali थुक्नु (thuknu), Odia ଥୁକିବା (thukibā). By surface analysis, تُھوک (thūk, “spit; saliva”) + ـنا (-nā, verb-forming suffix).

def: v.n. To be knocked, hammered, beaten; to be punished or chastised; to be defeated, overcome, worsted; to be thrown on the back (in wrestling); to suffer a loss; to be put in the stocks; to be imprisoned
s.m. corr. of ṭukṛā, q.v. = H ٿکر टुकर ṭukar, See ٿکڙ ṭukaṛ and ٿکڙا ṭukṛā.
more: s. m. A bit, a piece (of bread, &c.). See ٿکرا ṭukrā.

def: n. f. 1. Lordship. Niāo na kīn, kīn ṭhakurāī, Binā kin likh dīn burāī! Padmāvat. No justice dealt, but only sovereign power shewn, No act yet done, yet evil in man's fate set down. 2. The office of a barber.

ety: ṭhukrā˚ = ṭhokar, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. increment
def: v.t. To kick against, to strike the foot against ( = ṭhokar mārnā); to kick, trample upon; to spurn.
more: (from ٿهوکر) v. a. To kick or strike with the toes.

def: v. a. 1. To kick or touch with the foot. 2. To trample upon; spurn.
v.n. To spit (especially on hearing the name of a disease for the purpose of warding it off, or for the purpose of averting an evil eye).
v. a. Caus. of ṭhuknā , q. v.
v.t. To cause to be beaten, or hammered, &c.
s. (dakh.) Name of a dish or sweetmeat ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
adv. Falling gently, dropping, trickling (as water from a small height; — cf. ćul-ćul-ānā).
more: adv. Gently falling or dropping (as water from a small height). See چلچل chulchul.

def: adj. Of low stature, stunted, stumpy, short, diminutive; dapper
more: (from ٿهمکنا) s. f. The act of waving or walking with a graceful easy air. ٿهمک چال ṭhumak-chāl, A dignified pace, stately gait, graceful carriage.
subs: s.f. Mincing gait or air, affected walk; stately or dignified gait, strut.

def: v. n. To walk with a jerky, mincing, or wanton gait. ṭhumak ṭhumak , adj. Mincing; wanton. 1. Ṭhumak ṭhumak piyārī Rādhā nācheṅ, chandr badan jahāṅ champaī raṅg; Thaī thaī Krishn jī nācheṅ let tān jahāṅ tanan tanan . 2. Jān merī janyā, tū niklī kidhar se, Chāl chale ṭhumak ṭhumak patlī kamar se . Boys' rhyme. ṭhumak chāl , n. f. A mincing air; a wanton gait; a strut. Ho gaī ḍhaḍḍo, ṭhumak chāl kaise! Prov. Old grown, and mincing ugly shown!
v.n. To walk with a jerky, mincing, or wanton gait; to walk affectedly; to walk with grace, stateliness, or dignity; to strut, stalk.
more: v. n. To walk with grace and stateliness, to strut, to walk with dignity.

def: s.f. = ṭhumrī, q.v. s.f. A species of metre; a variety of song, a species of amorous composition.
adj. Of low stature, stunted, stumpy, short, diminutive; dapper
subs: s.f. Mincing gait or air, affected walk; stately or dignified gait, strut.

def: v. a. 1. To give a jerk (to a paper kite). 2. To hammer; beat in.
v.t. To cause to move or walk with stateliness, dignity, or grace; to cause to strut; to give a jerk to, to jerk (a paper kite); to make stunted; to hammer or beat down or in.
more: (caus. of ٿهمکنا) v. a. To cause to strut or march with dignity.

def: adj. & s.m. Idle, lazy; — a lazy fellow.
n. f. The play, or jerking of a paper kite to keep it up when the wind is light. ṭhumkī lagānā, yā denā , v. a. See ṭhumkānā , 1. Kankavve ko ṭhumke dīë jāo, nahīṅ gir paṛegā .
s.f. Jerking or playing (a paper kite, to keep it up when the wind is light)
more: 1. adj. Lazy. 2. s. f. The playing of a paper kite to keep it up when the wind is light.
subs: ṭhumkī lagānā (-ko), To give a jerk (to), to jerk (a paper kite) = ṭhumkānā.

def: adj. Small, short, &c. ( = ṭhumkā).
Mah. adj. Small; short; of low stature.
s.f. A species of metre; a variety of song, a species of amorous composition.

n. f. 1. A kind of verse. To this is sung a verse of two lines, as follows: 6 + 4 + 4 + 2, + 4 + 4 + 4 = 28 instants, or 6 + 4 + 4 + 2, + 6 + 4 + 4 + 2 = 32 instants, with pause in either at the 16th instant. Kellogg. 2. ( Slang ). A rumour ( avāī ).

ety: S. स्तोम or स्थूणा (with व inserted)+कः
def: s.m. A lump of earth (put on the end of a lever as a balance).

v.t. To strike with the beak, to peck, &c. = ṭūṅgnā; ṭhuṅgernā, qq.v. v.t. = ṭūṅgārnā, q.v. v.t. To pluck (fruit, &c.); to peck, to nibble; to pick, piddle, eat squeamishly or with distate; to gather up (the dress).
s.f. Pecking; picking, eating squeamishly, or bit by bit ( = ṭungār, q.v.; c.f. also ṭūṅg and ṭhoṅg).
n. f. H. ṭhong a beak. The act of picking; eating bit by bit. Terī ṭhuṅger ko holā hai . Dehli street cry.
v. a. To pick; peck; eat slowly.
v.t. To peck; to pick, to eat squeamishly, to piddle.

v.t. To strike with the beak, to peck, &c. = ṭūṅgnā; ṭhuṅgernā, qq.v. v.t. = ṭūṅgārnā, q.v. v.t. To pluck (fruit, &c.); to peck, to nibble; to pick, piddle, eat squeamishly or with distate; to gather up (the dress).

v. n. 1. To peck; strike with the beak. 2. To eat very slowly. 1. Baiṭhe baiṭhe ser bhar chane ṭhūṅg gayā . 2. Chaṛhī jo Shaikh ko afyūṅ, to dāna-i-tasbīh, Samajh ilāëchī-dāne tamām ṭhūṅg gayā . Nazīr. The opium to the priest's head got, he heeded none, His beads for cardamums took, he ate them every one.
s.f. (local) = thūnī, q.v.
s.f. Pillar, column, post, prop, the upright post over the mouth of a well.
more: n. f. S. स्थूणा A post; prop; the upright post over a well's mouth.
s. f. A post, pillar, column.

def: s. (dakh.) Spittle: see تهوک thūk.
v.t. = ṭhukwānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be beaten, or hammered, &c.
more: (caus. of ٿهونکنا) v. a. To cause to knock or hammer.

def: v. a. (dakh.) To spit: see تهوکنا thūknā.

ety: caus. and doub. caus. of ṭhūṅsnā
def: v.t. = ṭhusānā; ṭhuswānā, q.v. v.t. = ṭhuswānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to stuff or cram; to have (one) stuffed or crammed (with).

ety: ṭhoṅs˚ or ṭhos˚ = Prk. टुच्छ(इ) = S. त्वक्ष(ति)
def: v.t. To press down (or in) firm or hard, to beat (or drive, or force) in, to stuff, cram, thrust in; to wad; to eat greedily, to devour
v. a. 1. To cram; wad; thrust, force, or drive in. 2. To stuff oneself; to eat a great deal; to eat to bursting. Kyā tū hī sārī roṭiyāṅ ṭhūṅs jāëgā? Wom.

ety: caus. and doub. caus. of ṭhūṅsnā
def: v.t. = ṭhusānā; ṭhuswānā, q.v. v.t. = ṭhuswānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to stuff or cram; to have (one) stuffed or crammed (with).
s.m. = ṭhūṅṭh, q.v.

n. m. H. ṭūṭā broken. 1. A broken, leafless branch of a tree; a stump. 2. A stunted plant. 3. The bare trunk of a tree. Aë bādshāh ke laṛke, ham bāg-bāṅ tumhāre, Is ṭhunṭ ko hilā lo, jitne gireṅ tumhāre . Boys' rhyme. 4. The forearm up to the wrist.
s. m. See ٿهونٿهـ ṭhūnṭh.
s.m. Stump (of an arm, branch, &c.), branch (of a tree) broken off at the end and leafless; an amputated hand or arm; a stunted tree.
adj. Reduced to a stump, having the hand (or arm, &c.) amputated; having its branches lopped and leafless (a tree).
more: adj. 1. Having the hand amputated. 2. Having its branches lopped and leafless (a tree).

def: s.f. A small stump; a stalk; stubble.
adj. Having the hand, or arm, &c. amputated; mutilated ( = ṭhūṅṭhā, q.v.); — s.m. A eunuch.
adj. Having the hand, or arm, &c. amputated; mutilated ( = ṭhūṅṭhā, q.v.); — s.m. A eunuch.
more: 1. adj. See ٿهونٿها ṭhūnṭh-ā. 2. s. m. An eunuch.

def: s.m. The post which acts as a support to the ḍheklī.
more: n. m. The posts which act as supports to the ḍheklī .

ety: caus. of thopnā, q.v.; — thupā˚ = Prk. थुप्पावे˚ = S. स्तूप्य˚, rt. स्तूप्+आपि
def: v.t. To cause to prop, &c.; to cause to be propped, &c.
more: (caus. of تهوپنا q. v.) v. a. To cause to be supported, propped or plastered.

def: v.t. To bear, endure, suffer.
adj. P. dil heart. 1. Cowardly ( buz-dilā ). Thuṛ-dilā gīdaṛ se ḍar bhāgā . 2. Miserly; narrow; close. Baṛe thuṛ-dile se pālā paṛā .
adj. Faint-hearted, cowardly; narrow-hearted, miserly, close.
more: (تهوڙا Little, and دل Heart) adj. Miserly, narrow-minded.

def: s.f. Scarcity, lack.
s.f. Parched grain ( = ṭhuḍḍī, q.v.). s.f. The chin; — parched grain; — a spickle or head of Indian corn and the like, after the grains have been picked out, a cob
subs: (or ṭhuḍḍī) pakaṛnā (-kī), lit. 'To take hold of the chin'; to chuck under the chin; to humour, to flatter, to curry favour (with); to coax; to beg; to mollify; to appease
s.m. The dimple in a chin.

def: n. f. 1. Lack. See تورَا 1. 2. Dishonor; shame; ignominy; infamy. Thuṛī hai terī zāt bunyād par! Amānat. I spit on you and all your race! thuṛī thuṛī honā , v. n. To be dishonored, disgraced. Ābrū khūb āp kī hogī! Sab jahāṅ meṅ thuṛī thuṛī hogī . Wom. Shauq.

r: thūṛī, thoṛī, thū, ṛī, ṛe, aṛe
ety: thū, q.v.+ṛī, fem. of ṛe or aṛe, q.v.
def: intj. Ah!, fie!, for shame! (see thuṛī).
s.f. Spitting (at or on, -par, in scorn); disgrace, dishonour, shame, infamy (used chiefly by women); — intj. (express. of disgust, &c.), Ah! fie! for shame!
v.t. To strike, beat, injure, ruin, destroy, consume, devour.
more: v. n. 1. E. To beat; hammer; pound. 1. Gariaībā to thurbo karab! Mag. If you abuse me I'll strike you. 2. Darojā par pāoṅ dhailā to gatar gatar thur deb! Bhoj. (The husband to his wife). If you ever set foot outside the threshold, I'll break every bone in your body. 2. E. To prick; goad. Hārūṅ to hūrūṅ, jītūṅ to thūrūṅ . Prov. If I lose I'll hammer you, if I win I'll hammer you. ('He who's convinced against his will is of the same opinion still'). 3. W. To fall upon; eat up; devour ( khānā ). Āp hī āp thūr thār-ke baiṭh rahe .

def: v. n. H. thoṛā little. To be insufficient or wanting; to fail; fall short of. Khānā thuṛ gayā .
v.n. To fall short, be insufficient; to be wanting, to fail, be lacking
s.f. (prov.) = ṭhuḍḍī, q.v. s.f. The chin; — parched grain; — a spickle or head of Indian corn and the like, after the grains have been picked out, a cob
subs: (or ṭhuḍḍī) pakaṛnā (-kī), lit. 'To take hold of the chin'; to chuck under the chin; to humour, to flatter, to curry favour (with); to coax; to beg; to mollify; to appease
s.m. The dimple in a chin.

ety: ṭhusak˚ = Prk. टुच्छक्के(इ), fr. S. त्वक्ष+कृ
def: v.n. To weep silently or softly; to sob ( = ṭusaknā); peditum ciere, suppedere.
more: v. a. 1. Peditum ciere, suppedere. 2. To weep but not aloud.

def: v.t. = ṭhuswānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to stuff or cram; to have (one) stuffed or crammed (with).
more: (caus. of ٿهسنا) v. a. To cause to stuff.

def: s.f. Ventris crepitus surdus.
n. f. Ventris crepitus surdus .
v. n. To be stuffed, crammed. Sab ke sab isī darjeh meṅ ṭhus gaë .
v.n. To be filled full, to be stuffed, or crammed.
more: v. n. To be stuffed, to be crammed.

def: v. a. Caus. of تَهوسنا q. v.
v.t. To cause to stuff or cram; to have (one) stuffed or crammed (with).

ety: thoth+nā or nī = Prk. डओ or डिआ = S. ल+कः or इका
def: = H تهوتهنا थोथना thothnā, थुथना thuthnā, s.m. = H تهوتهني थोथनी thothnī, थुथनीthuthnī, s.f. The mouth or snout (of an animal, as a horse, camel, or hog, &c).
v.n. To distort (the mouth), to hang or turn down the lip (in disgust, &c.), to pout; to turn away (from, in disgust, &c.); to frown, scowl
more: Mah. Wom. v. a. To be sulky; to pout; make a wry face. Mūṅh thuthāë baiṭhī hai .
v. a. To frown, scowl or pout.
subs: To make a wry face, to pout, &c.

def: s.f. = thūthnī or thothnī, q.v.
more: s. f. The mouth of a horse, camel, &c.

def: To mock, scoff, revile.
s.m. The mouth or snout (of an animal); the mouth or face (of a human being: see thoth).
s.m. Making the sound thut in spitting; sputtering, spitting; — cholera morbus (in the language of Mohammadan women).
s.f. A sputtering hag; a hag, witch
more: Mah. Wom. n. f. A hag; witch ( balā ). Kyoṅ chaṛhī ātī hai, thutkārī sī sir par, bāṅdī, Bhūt lipṭā hai jo kartī nahīṅ, murdār, lihāz? Wom. Jān Sāhab. O slave! how like a harridan thou doth bear on me durst! No reverence thou hast, by fiend possessed base wretch accursed! thutkār' yāṅ , Mah. Wom. nf. 1. Fetters ( beṛyāṅ ). Bāz āve apnī fitrat se woh tab tak zikr kyā, Pāoṅ meṅ tab tak na kokā ke paṛeṅ thutkāryāṅ . Wom. Rangīn. 2. Shoes; slippers (Lucknow).
subs: (for the leg); a shoe, slipper (local, used chiefly by women).

def: v.n. To spit, to sputter; to spit or hiss (as a snake, &c.); to spit (at), to drive away scornfully; — to imitate the sound and action of spitting (in order to avert evil from one)
more: Wom. v. n. S. थुत्कार To imitate the sound and action of spitting in order to ward off any evil. Nanne ko thuthkār le, sir par pallā paṛ gayā . Hin. Wom.
v. n. To drive away scorn fully.
subs: v.t. To make the sound thut, as of spitting, over (a person or thing, in order to avert evil).

def: s.m. = thuthṛā, q.v. s.m. The mouth or snout (of an animal); the mouth or face (of a human being: see thoth).
adj. Three (common in Braj; but in Hindī and Urdū used chiefly as first member of compounds, e.g. ti-bārā, 'thrice'; ti-guṇ, 'three-fold').
more: (in Braj) Three: also, inflect. of the third pers. pron. sing. That, him, her, it.

def: adj. Three (in comp. e.g. ti-bārā, q.v.).
more: s. f. A woman, a wife.

more: adj. Unemployed; out of employment. 1. Bheḍ, bākrī le-kar, lalvā, jā tauṅ beg charāvan kū, Ṭhālī baiṭhe, mere bīrā, koū na degā khāvan kū . Rus. Wom. song. The sheep and goats, my son, take out to graze, and quickly go, While sitting idly, brother dear, no one will feed you so. 2. Are ṭhālī chūṅ baiṭṭhā sai, lavāre ne le jā nā? Rus. What are you idling there for? why dont you take out the young cattle? 3. Terī dhī baiṭhī sai ṭhālī, us nūṅ kār lagāve nā, Sovan saṅkh bajā rī sāsū, ab tauṅ kār karāve nā! Rus. Wom. song. Your daughter's sitting idle there, why dont you make her work? Now let me sleep awhile, dont, mother-in-law, keep me a-work.! ṭhāl'ī , adv. At leisure. ṭhālī honā, yā phirnā , v. n. To wander about; to saunter; to be unemployed.
